volumeTitle: Birds. Lysistrata. Women at the Thesmophoria
label: Birds (Section 1.1585b - Section 1.1683)

sections.label: Section 1.1585b
sections.original: εἶτα δῆτα σίλφιον
sections.translation: And you are going to season them before answering us ?

sections.label: Section 1.1586b
sections.original: ὦ χαῖρʼ Ἡράκλεις .
sections.translation: Ah ! Heracles ! welcome , welcome ! What ' s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 1.1587b
sections.original: πρεσβεύοντες ἡμεῖς ἥκομεν
sections.translation: The gods have sent us here as ambassadors to treat for peace .

sections.label: Section 1.1589
sections.original: ἔλαιον οὐκ ἔνεστιν ἐν τῇ ληκύθῳ .
sections.translation: There ' s no more oil in the flask .

sections.label: Section 1.1590
sections.original: καὶ μὴν τά γʼ ὀρνίθεια λιπάρʼ εἶναι πρέπει .
sections.translation: And yet the birds must be thoroughly basted with it .

sections.label: Section 1.1591
sections.original: ἡμεῖς τε γὰρ πολεμοῦντες οὐ κερδαίνομεν ,
sections.translation: We have no interest to serve in fighting you ; as for you , be friends and we promise that you shall always have rain-water in your pools and the warmest of warm weather .

sections.label: Section 1.1595
sections.original: τούτων περὶ πάντων αὐτοκράτορες ἥκομεν .
sections.translation: So far as these points go we are plenipotentiaries .

sections.label: Section 1.1596
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔτε πρότερον πώποθʼ ἡμεῖς ἤρξαμεν
sections.translation: We have never been the aggressors , and even now we are as well disposed for peace as yourselves , provided you agree to one equitable condition , namely ,

sections.label: Section 1.1600
sections.original: τὸ σκῆπτρον ἡμῖν τοῖσιν ὄρνισιν πάλιν
sections.translation: that Zeus yield his scepter to the birds . If only this is agreed to , I invite the ambassadors to dinner .

sections.label: Section 1.1603
sections.original: ἐμοὶ μὲν ἀπόχρη ταῦτα καὶ ψηφίζομαι .
sections.translation: That ' s good enough for me . I vote for peace .

sections.label: Section 1.1604
sections.original: τί ὦ κακόδαιμον ; ἠλίθιος καὶ γάστρις εἶ .
sections.translation: You wretch ! you are nothing but a fool and a glutton .

sections.label: Section 1.1605
sections.original: ἀποστερεῖς τὸν πατέρα τῆς τυραννίδος ;
sections.translation: Do you want to dethrone your own father ?

sections.label: Section 1.1606
sections.original: ἄληθες ; οὐ γὰρ μεῖζον ὑμεῖς οἱ θεοὶ
sections.translation: What an error . Why , the gods will be much more powerful if the birds govern the earth . At present the mortals are hidden beneath the clouds , escape your observation , and commit perjury in your name ;

sections.label: Section 1.1610
sections.original: ἐὰν δὲ τοὺς ὄρνις ἔχητε συμμάχους ,
sections.translation: but if you had the birds for your allies , and a man , after having sworn by the crow and Zeus , should fail to keep his oath , the crow would dive down upon him unawares and pluck out his eye .

sections.label: Section 1.1614
sections.original: νὴ τὸν Ποσειδῶ ταῦτά γέ τοι καλῶς λέγεις .
sections.translation: Well thought of , by Poseidon !

sections.label: Section 1.1615
sections.original: κἀμοὶ δοκεῖ .
sections.translation: My notion too .

sections.label: Section 1.1615b
sections.original: τί δαὶ σὺ φῄς ;
sections.translation: And you , what ' s your opinion ?

sections.label: Section 1.1615c
sections.original: ναβαισατρεῦ .
sections.translation: Nabasatreu .

sections.label: Section 1.1616
sections.original: ὁρᾷς ; ἐπαινεῖ χοὖτος . ἕτερόν νυν ἔτι
sections.translation: D ' you see ? he also approves . But listen , here is another thing in which we can serve you . If a man vows to offer a sacrifice to some god , and then procrastinates ,

sections.label: Section 1.1620
sections.original: μενετοὶ θεοί , καὶ μἀποδιδῷ μισητίᾳ ,
sections.translation: pretending that the gods can wait , and thus does not keep his word , we shall punish his stinginess .

sections.label: Section 1.1621b
sections.original: φέρʼ ἴδω τῷ τρόπῳ ;
sections.translation: Ah ! and how ?

sections.label: Section 1.1622
sections.original: ὅταν διαριθμῶν ἀργυρίδιον τύχῃ
sections.translation: While he is counting his money or is in the bath , a kite will relieve him , before he knows it , either in coin or in clothes ,

sections.label: Section 1.1625
sections.original: προβάτοιν δυοῖν τιμὴν ἀνοίσει τῷ θεῷ .
sections.translation: of the value of a couple of sheep , and carry it to the god .

sections.label: Section 1.1626
sections.original: τὸ σκῆπτρον ἀποδοῦναι πάλιν ψηφίζομαι
sections.translation: I vote for restoring them the scepter .

sections.label: Section 1.1627b
sections.original: καὶ τὸν Τριβαλλόν νυν ἐροῦ .
sections.translation: Ask Triballus .

sections.label: Section 1.1628
sections.original: ὁ Τριβαλλός , οἰμώζειν δοκεῖ σοι ;
sections.translation: Hi ! Triballus , do you want a thrashing ?

sections.label: Section 1.1628b
sections.original: σαυνάκα
sections.translation: Sure , bashum head withum stick .

sections.label: Section 1.1629b
sections.original: φησί μʼ εὖ λέγειν πάνυ .
sections.translation: He says , Right willingly .

sections.label: Section 1.1630
sections.original: εἴ τοι δοκεῖ σφῷν ταῦτα , κἀμοὶ συνδοκεῖ .
sections.translation: If that be the opinion of both of you , why , I consent too .

sections.label: Section 1.1631
sections.original: οὗτος , δοκεῖ δρᾶν ταῦτα τοῦ σκήπτρου πέρι .
sections.translation: Very well ! we accord you the scepter .

sections.label: Section 1.1632
sections.original: καὶ νὴ Δίʼ ἕτερόν γʼ ἐστὶν οὗ ʼμνήσθην ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Ah ! I was nearly forgetting another condition . I will leave Here to Zeus , but only if the young Basileia

sections.label: Section 1.1635
sections.original: ἐκδοτέον ἐστίν .
sections.translation: is given me in marriage .

sections.label: Section 1.1635b
sections.original: οὐ διαλλαγῶν ἐρᾷς .
sections.translation: Then you don ' t want peace . Let us withdraw .

sections.label: Section 1.1636b
sections.original: ὀλίγον μοι μέλει .
sections.translation: It matters mighty little to me . Cook , look to the gravy .

sections.label: Section 1.1638
sections.original: ὦ δαιμόνιʼ ἀνθρώπων Πόσειδον ποῖ φέρει ;
sections.translation: What an odd fellow this Poseidon is ! Where are you off to ? Are we going to war about a woman ?

sections.label: Section 1.1640
sections.original: τί δαὶ ποιῶμεν ;
sections.translation: What else is there to do ?

sections.label: Section 1.1640b
sections.original: ὅ τι ; διαλλαττώμεθα .
sections.translation: What else ? Why , conclude peace .

sections.label: Section 1.1641
sections.original: τί δʼ ᾠζύρʼ ; οὐκ οἶσθʼ ἐξαπατώμενος πάλαι ;
sections.translation: Oh ! you blockhead ! do you always want to be fooled ? Why , you are seeking your own downfall . If Zeus were to die , after having yielded them the sovereignty , you would be ruined , for you are the heir of

sections.label: Section 1.1645
sections.original: τὰ χρήμαθʼ , ὅσʼ ἂν ὁ Ζεὺς ἀποθνῄσκων καταλίπῃ .
sections.translation: all the wealth he will leave behind .

sections.label: Section 1.1646
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλας οἷόν σε περισοφίζεται .
sections.translation: Oh ! by the gods ! how he is cajoling you . Step aside , that I may have a word with you . Your uncle is getting the better of you , my poor friend .

sections.label: Section 1.1650
sections.original: κατὰ τοὺς νόμους · νόθος γὰρ εἶ κοὐ γνήσιος .
sections.translation: The law will not allow you an obolus of the paternal property , for you are a bastard and not a legitimate child .

sections.label: Section 1.1651
sections.original: ἐγὼ νόθος ; τί λέγεις ;
sections.translation: I a bastard ! What ' s that you tell me ?

sections.label: Section 1.1651b
sections.original: σὺ μέντοι νὴ Δία
sections.translation: Why , certainly ; are you not born of a stranger woman ? Besides , is not Athena recognized as Zeus ' sole heiress ? And no daughter would be that , if she had a legitimate brother .

sections.label: Section 1.1655
sections.original: τί δʼ ἢν ὁ πατὴρ ἐμοὶ διδῷ τὰ χρήματα
sections.translation: But what if my father wished to give me his property on his death-bed , even though I be a bastard ?

sections.label: Section 1.1656b
sections.original: ὁ νόμος αὐτὸν οὐκ ἐᾷ .
sections.translation: The law forbids it , and this same Poseidon would be the first to lay claim to his wealth , in virtue of being his legitimate brother . Listen ;

sections.label: Section 1.1660
sections.original: ἐρῶ δὲ δὴ καὶ τὸν Σόλωνός σοι νόμον ·
sections.translation: thus runs Solon ' s law : A bastard shall not inherit , if there are

sections.label: Section 1.1665
sections.original: γνησίων . ἐὰν δὲ παῖδες μὴ ὦσι γνήσιοι , τοῖς
sections.translation: legitimate children ; and if there are no legitimate children , the property shall pass to the nearest kin .

sections.label: Section 1.1667
sections.original: ἐμοὶ δʼ ἄρʼ οὐδὲν τῶν πατρῴων χρημάτων
sections.translation: And I get nothing whatever of the paternal property ?

sections.label: Section 1.1668b
sections.original: οὐ μέντοι μὰ Δία . λέξον δέ μοι ,
sections.translation: Absolutely nothing . But tell me , has your father had you entered on the registers of his phratry ?

sections.label: Section 1.1670
sections.original: οὐ δῆτʼ ἐμέ γε . καὶ δῆτʼ ἐθαύμαζον πάλαι .
sections.translation: No , and I have long been surprised at the omission .

sections.label: Section 1.1671
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἄνω κέχηνας αἴκειαν βλέπων ;
sections.translation: Why do you shake your fist at heaven ? Do you want to fight ? Why , be on my side , I will make you a king and will feed you on bird ' s milk and honey .

sections.label: Section 1.1674
sections.original: δίκαιʼ ἔμοιγε καὶ πάλαι δοκεῖς λέγειν
sections.translation: Your further condition seems fair to me .

sections.label: Section 1.1675
sections.original: περὶ τῆς κόρης , κἄγωγε παραδίδωμί σοι .
sections.translation: I cede you the young damsel .

sections.label: Section 1.1676b
sections.original: τἀναντία ψηφίζομαι .
sections.translation: But I , I vote against this opinion .

sections.label: Section 1.1677
sections.original: ἐν τῷ Τριβαλλῷ πᾶν τὸ πρᾶγμα . τί σὺ λέγεις ;
sections.translation: Then it all depends on the Triballus . To the Triballus . What do you say ?

sections.label: Section 1.1678
sections.original: καλάνι κόραυνα καὶ μεγάλα βασιλιναῦ
sections.translation: Givum bird pretty gel bigum queen .

sections.label: Section 1.1679b
sections.original: παραδοῦναι λέγει .
sections.translation: He says give her .

sections.label: Section 1.1680
sections.original: μὰ τὸν Δίʼ οὐχ οὗτός γε παραδοῦναι λέγει ,
sections.translation: Why no , he does not say anything of the sort , or else , like the swallows he does not know how to walk .

sections.label: Section 1.1682
sections.original: οὐκοῦν παραδοῦναι ταῖς χελιδόσιν λέγει .
sections.translation: Exactly so . Does he not say she must be given to the swallows ?

sections.label: Section 1.1683
sections.original: σφὼ νῦν διαλλάττεσθε καὶ ξυμβαίνετε ·
sections.translation: All right , you two arrange the matter ; make peace , since you wish it so ; I ' ll hold my tongue .
